figures.caption: FIG. 6. Scaled energies of the Y state and UDD state as functions of 1/∆. Open squares and filled circles denote the DMRG energies of the UDD and Y states, respectively, obtained from the local energy around the center of the system (ecenter/∆), while red crosses show the thermodynamic-limit energy obtained without assuming any particular order (etot/∆). The blue solid line denotes the strong-Ising expansion for the UDD state [Eq. (20)].

FIG. 2. (a) Schematic illustration of the 6×6 triangular-lattice cluster with spiral boundary conditions. The sites corresponding to the sublattice moment 2m1 are shown in orange, while those corresponding to −m2 are shown in blue. (b) One-dimensional mapping of the cluster in panel (a). In the actual calculation, open boundary conditions are imposed on the mapped chain. For numerical convenience, the chain is taken to contain sites up to L2 + 1. Large pinning fields are applied to sites 1, 4, L2 − 2, and L2 + 1 in order to fix their spins to Sz ≈ 1/2.

FIG. 8. Finite-size scaling of the net magnetization per site Mz(L,∆) as a function of 1/L. The solid curve denotes quadratic polynomial fit used for the extrapolation to the thermodynamic limit. The same qualitative behavior is found for all ∆ considered.

FIG. 7. Representative finite-size scaling plots of m1(L,∆) and m2(L,∆) as functions of 1/L for several values of ∆. The curves represent fits to the form m(L) = a + b/Lγ used to estimate the thermodynamic-limit values.

FIG. 9. Comparison of the sublattice magnetization mA of the UDD state between DMRG and perturbation theory (PT) as functions of 1/∆. The DMRG data are obtained in the magnetization sector Sz tot = −Ns/6, while the solid curve denotes the perturbative result, Eq. (C12).

FIG. 4. Anisotropy dependence of the positive and negative sublattice moments, 2m1 and m2, for the spin-1/2 triangular-lattice XXZ model. The data are extracted from the local magnetization profile using the procedure described in Sec. III C 1. The direct comparison between m1 and m2 is given in TABLE I.

TABLE I. Numerical estimates of the positive and negative sublattice moments, 2m1 and m2, for the spin-1/2 triangular-lattice XXZ model at several values of ∆. The last column shows the relative difference between m1 and m2.

FIG. 3. Local spin moment ⟨Sz i ⟩ for the projected 1D chain with 145 sites obtained from the original 12 × 12 triangular-lattice cluster at ∆ = 1.1. Pinning fields are applied at sites i = 1, 4, 7, 10, L2 − 8, L2 − 5, L2 − 2, and L2 + 1. (a) Profile over the entire system, i = 1–145. (b) Enlarged view of the central region, i = 50–100.
